metadata.name: Commander damage accumulates — 10 + 11 = 21 (kills player)
metadata.description: p1's commander has already dealt 10 combat damage to p2 in prior turns. This turn, p1's commander deals 11 more combat damage. p2 now has 21 commander damage from p1 (>= 21), and the SBA kills p2 (CR 704.6c). Tests that commander damage accumulates across turns and that the 21-damage threshold SBA triggers.
metadata.generation_notes: CR 903.10: A player who has been dealt 21 or more combat damage by the same commander loses the game. CR 704.6c: SBA checks commander damage. Initial state: p2 has received 10 commander damage from p1's commander. After 11 more damage (this turn's combat), total = 21. SBA fires: p2 loses. The initial state sets commander_damage_received for p2. The script models the final combat hit. Note: combat scripts are informational (draft) because the harness doesn't support declare attackers. The commander damage SBA behavior is tested in commander_damage.rs.

script.step_note: p1's commander Llanowar Elves deals 11 more commander damage to p2 (total = 21).

actions.description: Initial: p2 has 10 commander damage from p1 already
actions.note: p2 has received 10 prior commander damage from p1's commander.

actions.object: Combat damage step — commander hits p2

resolution.note: CR 903.10: p1's commander Llanowar Elves deals 11 combat damage to p2. Commander damage total: 10 + 11 = 21.

actions.note: Commander deals 11 combat damage; total commander damage from p1 = 21.
CR 704.6c: p2 has received 21 or more combat damage from p1's commander. p2 loses the game.
SBA: p2 has 21 commander damage from p1 — p2 loses.

actions.description: After SBA: p2 has lost the game (commander damage threshold)
actions.note: p2 received 21 commander damage from p1, triggering the loss SBA.
